title: leucanthemum atratum subsp. tridactylites
intro: Leucanthemum atratum subsp. tridactylites, also known as the Ox-eye Daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to Europe and western Asia. The medicinal properties of Leucanthemum atratum subsp. tridactylites include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Leucanthemum atratum subsp. tridactylites is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
